name: the-intellectual-life
description: A. D. Sertillanges' classic guide to building a life of serious study — organizing the mind, body, and soul for sustained intellectual work.
useCase: Discerning intellectual vocation, organizing a study system, digesting reading, sustaining creative output, handling solitude and burnout.
knownRisks: Written from a Catholic/Thomistic perspective — secular readers may need to translate the religious framing
The book's historical context (1920s France) includes class and gender assumptions that feel dated
Some practical advice (note-taking, classification) predates digital tools
